hadiths.ar:
4104  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْ هِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى   
رَّهْن ٍ إِج ْي َّلَّف َّ ق ِي هِ إَّلز َّإهِن ُّ وَّ إَّلْمُّزْي َّهِن ُّ ق َّق َّال لْمُّزْي َّهِن ُّ هُّوَّ ن ِا َّكْي َّزَّ إ ِن  َّهُّ ي ُّصَّذ َّق ُّ إَّلْمُّزْي َّهِن ُّ 
جَّي  َّى ي ُّحِي ظَّ ن ِالي  َّمَّن ِ لِأ َّن  َّهُّ إ َّمِي ن
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s}: 
"If a man pledges ( rahn) a piece of land that contains fruit, then its produce shall be 
accounted as part of his payment. He is also entitled to calculate what he worked on 
and spent on it. Once his debt is settled, he must return the land to its owner."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.308 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.849 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.18 
p.396 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4103
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٣

hadiths.ar:
4105  -  وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى عَّن ْ إ ِسْحَّاق َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ي َّكُّون ُّ عِن ْذَّةُّ إَّلز َّهْن ُّ 
ق َّلاَّ ي َّذْرِى  لِمَّن ْ هُّوَّ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّاسِ ق َّق َّال ن ْ هُّوَّ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّاسِ 
ق َّق َّال   كَّان َّ ق ِي هِ ق َّص ْل  إ َّوْ ن ُّق ْصَّان   مَّا ي َّصْن َّغُّ ق َّال 
ي َّن ِي عُّهُّ ق َّي ُّو ْج َّزُّ ن ِمَّا ن َّق ِى َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق ِي هِ ق َّص ْل  ق َّهُّوَّ إ َّس َّذ ُّهُّمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِ ي َّن ِي عُّهُّ وَّ ي ُّمْسِك ُّ ق َّص ْلَّهُّ جَّي  َّ ى ي َّح ِى ءَّ صَّاجِن ُّه  
ق َّالَّ مُّصَّن ِّف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إلْكِي َّاب ِ رَّجِمَّهُّ إلل َّهُّ هَّذ َّإ إ ِذ َّإ لَّمْ ي َّعْزِف ْ صَّاجِن َّهُّ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّطْمَّغْ ق ِى  رُّج ُّوعِهِ ق َّمَّي َّى  عَّزَّف َّ صَّاجِن َّهُّ ق َّلَّي ْسَّ لَّهُّ ن َّي ْعُّهُّ جَّي  َّى  
ي َّح ِى  ءَّ وَّ ي َّصْذِي ق ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ.
hadiths.en:
Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} (peace be upon them), who said 
that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} stated: 
"In a case of dispute over a pledged item ( rahn) between the pledger ( rahin) and the 
pledgee ( murtahin), where the pledger claims its value is such -and-such, and the 
pledgee claims it is of a higher value, the pledgee shall be believed until the value 
covers the debt because he is considered trustworthy." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.308 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.175 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 
p.122 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.236 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.865 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.403 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4104
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٤

hadiths.ar:
4106  -  مَّا رَّوَّإةُّ إَّلْق َّاسِمُّ ن ْن ُّ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ عَّن ْ عُّن َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  ر ج ُّلٍ رَّهَّن َّ رَّهْن اً إ ِلَّى  
وَّق ْث ٍ ي ُّم َّ ع َّاب َّ هَّلْ لَّهُّ وَّق ْث   ي ُّن َّاعُّ ق ِي هِ رَّهْن ُّهُّ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said: 
"I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s}) about a man who has a 
pledged item (rahn) in his possession but does not know to whom it belongs.  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘I do not recommend that he sell it until its owner comes.'  
I said: ‘He does not know to whom it belongs.'  
Imam {a.s} replied: ‘Does it have any excess value or deficiency?'  
I said: ‘What if it has excess or deficiency, what should he do?'  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘If it has a deficiency, it is easier; he may sell it and be rewarded for what 
remains. However, if it has excess value, that is more difficult; he should sell it and 
hold the surplus until its owner comes.' " 
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, commented: 
"This applies if the owner is unknown and there is no expectation of his return. 
However, if the owner is identified, it is not permissible to sell it until the owner 
comes." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.233 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.309 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.168 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.851 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.384 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4105
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٥

hadiths.ar:
4107   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ عُّن َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ُّل  رَّهَّن َّ عِن ْذ  رَّج ُّلٍ  سِوَّإرَّي ْن ِ ق َّهَّلَّك َّ  
إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-Qasim ibn Sulaiman narrated from Ubayd ibn Zurara, who reported  from 
Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} regarding a man who pledged a 
security (rahn) for a specified time and then disappeared, should there be a time set 
to sell his pledge?" 
Imam {a.s} replied: "No, it should not be sold until he returns." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.309 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.169 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.852 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.384 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4106
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٦

hadiths.ar:
4108   -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ رَّهَّن َّ عِن ْذَّ رَّج ُّلٍ ذَّإرإً ق َّاجْي َّزَّق َّث ْ إ َّوِ إِن ْهَّذَّمَّث ْ ق َّال 
إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Ubayd ibn Zurara, who said:  
"I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who pledged two bracelets ( siwarayn) as 
security with another man, and one of them was destroyed." 
Imam {a.s} replied: "He may reclaim his right from what remains." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.309 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4107
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٧

hadiths.ar:
4109  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ رَّهَّن َّ عِن ْذَّةُّ رَّج ُّل  مَّمْلُّوكاً ق َّح ُّذ ِمَّ إ َّوْ رَّهَّن َّ عِن ْذَّةُّ مَّي َّاعاً ق َّلَّمْ ي ن ْس ُّزْ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْمَّي َّاعَّ 
وَّ لَّمْ ي َّي َّعَّاهَّذْةُّ وَّ لَّمْ ي ُّحَّزِّكْهُّ ق َّا ُّكِلَّ ي َّعْن ِى  إ َّكَّلَّهُّ إَّلس ُّوسُّ هَّلْ ي َّن ْق ُّضُّ مِن ْ مَّالِهِ ن ِق َّذْرِ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said regarding a man who pledged a house as security with another 
man, and the house either burned down or collapsed: "His wealth remains in the soil 
of the land." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.309 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4108
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٨

hadiths.ar:
4110  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّزْهَّن عِن ْذَّ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إَّلز َّهْن َّ ق َّي ُّصِي ن ُّهُّ 
ي َّوًى إ َّوْ ص َّاعَّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} was asked about a man who had pledged a slave as security, and 
the slave developed leprosy, or about a man who pledged goods as security but did 
not inspect, maintain, or move those goods, and they were consumed by worms.  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, it does not diminish from his wealth by that amount."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.309 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.856 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4109
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٠٩

hadiths.ar:
4111   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ عِي سَّى ن ْن ِ عُّن َّي ْذٍ عَّن ْ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ ن ْن ِ جَّق ْضٍ إَّلْمَّزْوَّر ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : كَّي َّن ْث ُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ مَّاب َّ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ذَّي ْن   وَّ لَّمْ ي ُّح َّلِّف ْ س َّي ْن اً إ ِلأ َّ رَّهْن اً ق ِى  ي َّذِ ن َّعْص ِهِمْ وَّ لأَّ ي َّن ْلُّغ ُّ  ي َّمَّن ُّهُّ إ َّكْي َّزَّ مِن ْ مَّالِ إَّلْمُّزْي َّهِن ِ  
إ َّ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّةُّ ن ِمَّالِهِ  إ َّوْ هُّوَّ وَّ سَّان ِزُّ إَّلذ ُّي  َّان ِ ق ِي هِ س ُّزَّكَّاءُّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم وَّر ِّعُّون َّهُّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّمْ 
ن ِالْحِصَّضِ  مَّالأً وَّ إ َّن  َّ عِن ْذَّةُّ رَّهْن اً ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّم  لْي َّزُّذ َّ إَّلْن َّاق ِى َّ عَّلَّى وَّرَّي َّي ِهِ وَّ مَّي َّى 
إ َّق َّز َّ ن ِمَّا عِن ْذَّةُّ إ ُّج ِذ َّ   ن ِهِ وَّ طُّولِث َّ ن ِالْن َّي ِّن َّه ِ عَّلَّى ذَّعْوَّإةُّ وَّ إ َّوْق َّى جَّق  َّهُّ ن َّعْذَّ إَّلْي َّمِي ن ِ وَّ مَّي َّى لَّمْ ي ُّق ِمِ إَّلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ وَّ إَّ لْوَّرَّي َّه ُّ مُّن ْكِزُّون َّ 
ق َّلَّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِمْ ي َّمِي ن ُّ عِلْمٍ ي َّحْلِق ُّون َّ ن ِالل َّهِ مَّا ي َّعْلَّمُّون َّ إ َّن  َّ لَّهُّ عَّلَّى مَّي ِّي ِهِمْ جَّق  اً .
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al -Halabi, from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, regarding a man who pledged a security with another man, 
and the security was destroyed or lost. 
Imam {a.s} said: "He may reclaim his money from him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.310 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4110
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٠

hadiths.ar:
4112  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ق َّص َّالَّه ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّكُّون ُّ إَّلز َّهْن ُّ ن ِمَّا ق ِي هِ 
إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ جَّي َّوَّإن اً إ َّوْ ذَّإن  َّه ً إ َّوْ ق ِص  َّه ً إ َّوْ مَّي َّاعاً ق َّا َّصَّان َّهُّ جَّزِي ق   إ َّوْ لُّصُّوض  ق َّهَّلَّك َّ مَّالُّهُّ إ َّوْ ن َّق َّضَّ  مَّي َّاعُّهُّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ لَّهُّ عَّلَّى 
مُّصِي ن َّي ِهِ ن َّي ِّن َّه   ق َّال   كُّل ُّهُّ ق َّلَّمْ ي ُّوج َّذْ لَّهُّ س َّى ْء  ق َّلاَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
وَّ لَّهُّ مَّال  ق َّلاَّ ي ُّصَّذ َّق
hadiths.en:
Muhammad bin Isa bin Ubaid narrated from Sulaiman bin Hafs Al -Marwazi, 
who said: I wrote to Abu Al -Hasan {a.s} about a man who died and had a debt, leaving 
nothing except a pledged item in the possession of someone. The value of the pledge 
did not exceed the creditor’s due amount. I asked whether the creditor could take it 
for his claim or if all creditors shared in it. 
Imam {a.s} wrote: "All creditors are equal in this matter and must distribute it among 
themselves in proportion to their claims." 
He further said: I wrote to Imam {a.s} about a man who died, leaving heirs. Then 
someone came forward, claiming a debt against him and that he held a pledge for it.  
Imam {a.s} wrote: "If he has a claim against the deceased but has no evidence for it, he 
may take his due from what is in his possession and return the remainder to the heirs. 
However, if he admits to what he possesses, he is bound by it and must provide 
evidence fo r his claim. He must fulfill his right after swearing an oath. If he cannot 
provide evidence and the heirs deny the claim, then the heirs must take an oath of 
knowledge, swearing by Allah {SWT} that they have no knowledge of any right owed to 
him by their deceased." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.310 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.178 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.844 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4111
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١١

hadiths.ar:
4113   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّجْمَّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصْزٍ إَّلْن َّز َّن ْطِى  ُّ عَّن ْ ذَّإوُّذَّ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحُّصَّي ْن ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّن  َّاسِ إَّلْق َّص ْلِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ  
إَّلْمَّلِك ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ رَّهَّن َّ عِن ْذَّةُّ إ ج َّزُّ عَّن ْذَّي ْن ِ ق َّ هَّلَّك َّ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا إ َّ ي َّكُّون ُّ جَّق  ُّهُّ ق ِى   
إَّلْأ ج َّزِ ق َّال ث ُّ إ َّوْ ذَّإرإً ق َّاجْي َّزَّق َّث ْ إ َّ ي َّكُّون ُّ جَّق  ُّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلي  ُّزْن َّه ِ ق َّال مَّا إ َّ 
ي َّكُّون ُّ جَّق  ُّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ ُّج ْزَّى ق َّال إ َّوْ ع ُّلاَّماً ق َّا َّصَّان َّهُّ ج ُّذَّرِى     
ق َّعَّمِى َّ إ َّوْ ي ِي َّان اً ي َّزَّكَّهَّا مَّطْوِي  َّه ً لَّمْ ي َّي َّعَّاهَّذْهَّا وَّ لَّمْ ي َّن ْس ُّزْهَّا جَّي  َّى هَّلَّكَّث ْ ق َّال ذٍ ي َّكُّون ُّ جَّق  ُّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Fadalah narrated from Aban, who narrated from a man, that Abu Abdillah {a.s} 
was asked: "How does the collateral (rahn) remain valid in cases where it is an animal, 
a mount, silver, or goods, and it gets destroyed due to fire or theft, causing the owner’s 
loss or a decrease in its value, and there is no evidence to support the claim of loss?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "If all of his goods are completely destroyed and nothing remains, then 
there is nothing (no liability) upon him." 
Imam {a.s} further said: "However, if he claims that the loss occurred from among his 
possessions while he still possesses other property, he will not be believed."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V.3 p.310 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, V.7 p.173 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.857 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4112
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٢

hadiths.ar:
4114 - وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى عَّن ْ إ ِسْحَّاق َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّزْهَّن إَّلز َّهْن َّ ن ِمِان َّه ِ 
ذِرْهَّمٍ وَّ هُّوَّ ي ُّسَّاوِى  ي َّلاَّي َّمِان َّه ِ ذِرْهَّمٍ  ق َّي ُّهْلِكُّهُّ إ َّ عَّلَّى إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إ َّن ْ ي َّزُّذ َّ عَّلَّى صَّاجِن ِهِ مِان َّي َّى ْ ذِرْهَّمٍ ق َّال 
رَّهْن اً ق ِي هِ ق َّص ْل  وَّ ص َّي  َّعَّه ق َّص ْلَّ ق َّال »
hadiths.en:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr al -Bazanti narrated from Dawud ibn al -
Husayn, who narrated from Abu al-Abbas al-Fadl ibn Abd al-Malik, who narrated from 
Abu Abdillah {a.s}: I asked him about a man who took two slaves as collateral (rahn) from 
another man, and one of them perished. Does his right apply to the other slave?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes." 
I asked: What if it was a house that burned down? Does his right remain in the land?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes." 
I asked: What if it was two animals and one of them perished? Does his right apply to 
the other? 
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes." 
I asked: What if it was merchandise that was destroyed because it was left for a long 
time, or food that spoiled, or a servant who became blind due to smallpox, or clothes 
that were left folded without being aired out until they were ruined?  
Imam {a.s} said: "All of this is the same case. His right remains applicable to the 
remaining property." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.311 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.389 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4113
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٣

hadiths.ar:
4115 - وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ ق َّي ْسٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لاَّمُّ ق ِى  إَّلز َّهْن ِ 
إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ إ َّكْي َّزَّ مِن ْ مَّالِ إَّلْمُّزْي َّهِن ِ ق َّهَّلَّك َّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّو َّذ َّى إَّلْق َّص ْلُّ إ ِلَّى صَّاجِث ِ إَّلز َّهْن ِ وَّ  إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ إَّلز َّهْن ُّ إ َّق َّل َّ مِن ْ مَّالِهِ ق َّهَّلَّك َّ 
إَّلز َّهْن ُّ إ َّذ َّى إ ِلَّي ْهِ صَّاجِن ُّهُّ ق َّص ْلَّ مَّالِهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ إَّلز َّهْن ُّ ي َّسْوَّى مَّا رَّهَّن َّهُّ ق َّلَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ س َّى ْء
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:  
I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa Al -Kadhim {a.s}) about a man who takes an item as 
collateral (rahn) for one hundred dirhams, while its value is three hundred dirhams, 
and it gets destroyed. Is the man required to return two hundred dirhams to its owner? 
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, because he took collateral that had surplus value and then 
caused its loss." 
I asked: What if half of the collateral is destroyed? 
Imam {a.s} said: "It will be accounted for accordingly." 
I asked: Should they return the surplus value between them?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.234 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.311 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.172 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 p.120 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.861 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.391  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4114
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٤

hadiths.ar:
4116  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ق َّص َّالَّه ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال إِج ْي َّلَّق َّا ق ِى  إَّلز َّهْن ِ ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا رَّهَّن ْي َّهُّ 
ن ِا َّلْف ِ ذِرْهَّمٍ وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلْأ ج َّزُّ رَّهَّن ْي ُّهُّ ن ِمِان َّه ِ ذِرْهَّمٍ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ي ُّسْا َّلُّ صَّاجِث ُّ إَّلْأ َّلْف ِ إَّلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ ق َّا ِن ْ   لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لَّهُّ ن َّي ِّن َّه   جَّلَّف َّ صَّاجِث ُّ 
إَّلْمِان َّه ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ إَّلز َّ هْن ُّ إ َّق َّل َّ مِم َّا رَّهَّن َّ ن ِهِ إ َّوْ إ َّكْي َّزَّ وَّ إِج ْي َّلَّق َّا ق ِى  إَّلز َّهْن ِ ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا هُّوَّ رَّهْن   وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلْأ ج َّ زُّ هُّوَّ وَّذِي عَّه   
ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ي ُّسْا َّلُّ صَّاجِث ُّ إَّلْوَّذِي عَّه ِ إَّلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ ق َّا ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لَّهُّ ن َّي ِّن َّه   جَّلَّف َّ صَّاجِث ُّ إَّلز َّهْ ن ِ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s}: 
"Commander of the Faithful {a.s} ruled regarding collateral (rahn) that if its value is 
greater than the debt owed to the creditor and it is destroyed, the excess value must 
be returned to the owner of the collateral. 
And if the collateral is worth less than the debt and is destroyed, the debtor must pay 
the remaining amount owed to the creditor. 
But if the value of the collateral equals the debt, then there is no further obligation on 
either party." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.312 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.392 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4115
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٥

hadiths.ar:
4117  -  وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى عَّن ْ إ ِسْحَّاق َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّزْهَّن ُّ 
إَّلْعَّن ْذَّ إ َّوِ إَّلي  َّوْب َّ إ َّوِ إَّلْحُّلِى  َّ إ َّوْ مَّي َّاعَّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ صَّاجِث ُّ إَّلْمَّي َّاعِ لِلْمُّزْي َّهِن ِ إ َّن ْث َّ ق ِى  جِ لٍّ مِن ْ لُّن ْسِ هَّذ َّإ إَّلي  َّوْب ِ إِلْن َّسِ  
إَّلي  َّوْب َّ وَّ إِن ْي َّق ِغْ ن ِالْمَّي َّاعِ وَّ إِسْي َّح ْذِمِ إَّلْح َّاذِمَّ ق َّ ال 
لَّهَّا ع َّل َّه   لِمَّن ِ إَّلْع َّل َّه ُّ ق َّال رْض ِ إِر ْرَّعْهَّا لِن َّق ْسِك َّ ق َّق َّ الَّ  
«هِ وَّ ي َّعْمُّزُّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Fadalah narrated from Aban, from Abu Abdillah {a.s}: 
"If two parties disagree about the value of collateral (rahn), with one claiming it was 
pledged for one thousand dirhams and the other claiming it was pledged for one 
hundred dirhams, the one claiming the higher value must present evidence. If he 
cannot provide evidence, the one claiming the lower value must take an oath."  
"Similarly, if the collateral is worth either less or more than the claimed amount, and 
they disagree about its status, whether it is collateral (rahn) or a deposit (wadi'ah), the 
one claiming it is a deposit must present evidence. If he cannot provide evi dence, the 
one claiming it is collateral must take an oath." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.237 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.312 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.174 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.863 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4116
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٦

hadiths.ar:
4118  - وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ رَّن َّاحٍ إَّلْق َّلا َّءِ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ هَّلَّك َّ  
إ َّج ُّوةُّ وَّ ي َّزَّك َّ صُّن ْذُّوق اً ق ِي هِ رُّهُّون   ن َّعْص ُّهَّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إِسْمُّ صَّاجِن ِهِ وَّ ن ِكَّمْ هُّوَّ رُّهِن َّ وَّ ن َّعْص ُّهَّا لأَّ ي ُّذْرَّى  لِمَّن ْ هُّوَّ وَّ لأَّ ن ِكَّمْ هُّوَّ 
رُّهِن َّ مَّا ي َّزَّى ق ِى  هَّذ َّإ إَّل َّذ ِى  لأَّ ي ُّ عْزَّف ُّ صَّاجِن ُّهُّ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:  
"I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s}) about a man who pledges a 
slave, garment, jewellery, or household items as collateral, and the owner of the 
property tells the creditor, 'You are permitted to use this garment, wear it, benefit from 
the property, and use the servant.'  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘It is lawful for him if the owner permits it, but I do not like him to do so.'" 
I then asked: ‘What if someone pledges a house that produces income, who does the 
income belong to?'  
Imam {a.s} replied: ‘The income belongs to the owner of the house.' "  
I further asked: ‘What if someone pledges a barren land and the owner of the land tells 
him to cultivate it for himself?'  
Imam {a.s} replied: ‘This is lawful. It is not the same as the previous case. He cultivates 
it with his own money, so it is lawful for him as the owner has permitted it, because he 
invests his own resources and develops the land.' " 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.235 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.312 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.173 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.847 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.392 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4117
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٧

hadiths.ar:
4119  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلْأ َّسَّذِى  ُّ رَّص ِى َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّن ْ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ إَّلن  َّح َّعِ ى ِّ عَّن ْ عَّمِّهِ 
إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ ن ْن ِ ي َّز ِي ذَّ  إَّلن  َّوْق َّلِى ِّ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْح َّ ن َّزِ إَّل َّذ ِى  رُّوِى َّ 
«إ ِذ َّإ ط َّهَّزَّ إَّلْحَّق  ُّ وَّ ق َّامَّ ق َّان ِمُّن َّا إ َّهْلَّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ 
»إَّلْحَّق  ُّ وَّ ق َّامَّ ق َّان ِمُّن َّا إ َّهْل 
إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ وَّ إ َّم َّا إَّلْي َّوْمَّ ق َّ لاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِا َّن ْ ي َّن ِي غَّ مِن َّ إَّلْأ َّح ِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِ وَّ ي َّزْن َّحَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Muhammad ibn Rabah al -Qalla’, who said: 
"I asked Abu al -Hasan {a.s} about a man whose brother passed away and left behind a 
box containing pledged items. Some of these items had the names of their owners and 
the amounts for which they were pledged, while others had no information about their 
owners or the amounts for whic h they were pledged. What is the ruling regarding the 
items whose owners are unknown?" 
Imam {a.s} replied: ‘They are treated as part of his property.' " 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.236 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.313 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.170 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.842 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.399 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.13 p.423 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4118
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٨

hadiths.ar:
4120   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّزْهَّن ُّ ج َّارِي َّي َّهُّ إ َّ 
ي َّحِل ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّطَّا َّهَّا ق َّال ق َّذَّرَّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا ج َّالِي اً وَّ لَّمْ ي َّ عْلَّمِ 
إَّل َّذ ِي ن َّ إِرْي َّهَّن ُّوهَّا ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Ja'far al-Asadi (may Allah {SWT} be pleased with 
him) narrated from Musa ibn Imran al -Nakha'i, from his uncle al -Husayn ibn Yazid al -
Nawfali, from Ali ibn Salim, from his father, who said:  
"I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about the report that has been narrated: 'Whoever relies 
more on a pledge (rahn) than on his believing brother, I am free from him.'  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'That applies when the truth becomes manifest, and our Qaim (the 
Mahdi Imam Muhammad ibn Hassan Al-Mahdi {a.s}) from among the Ahlulbayt {a.s} rises.' 
I then asked about the report that says: 'The profit earned by a believer from another 
believer is riba (usury).' 
Imam {a.s} said: 'That applies when the truth becomes manifest, and our Qaim {a.s} from 
among the Ahlulbayt {a.s} rises. As for today, there is no harm if a believer sells to his 
believing brother and earns profit from him.' " 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.313 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.178 • Ithbat Al-Huda, 
Vol.5 p.68 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4119
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١١٩

hadiths.en:
Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who reported from Abu Jafar 
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}: 
"I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who pledges his bondwoman (as collateral). Is it 
permissible for him to have intercourse with her?" 
Imam {a.s} replied: "Those who have taken her as collateral (the creditors) prevent him 
from having access to her." 
I then asked: "What if he manages to be with her privately without the knowledge of 
those who took her as collateral?" 
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, I do not see any harm in this." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.313 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.234

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 92, H. 4120
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٤١٢٠

hadiths.ar:
4122  -  وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّزَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إ َّكَّلَّ مِن ْهُّ إَّلْكَّلْث ُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ إ َّكَّلَّ مِن ْهُّ ي ُّلُّي َّي ْهِ كُّلْ مَّا إ َّكَّلَّ إَّلْكَّلْث ُّ 
وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّن ْق َّ مِن ْهُّ إ ِلأ َّ ن َّص ْعَّه   وَّإجِذَّة
hadiths.en:
Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurara,  from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, that he said: 
"Regarding the hunting of a dog, if its owner releases it and pronounces the name of 
Allah {SWT}, then he may eat whatever it catches for him, even if it kills. And if it eats from 
it, then eat what remains.  
If the dog is not trained, but the owner trains it at the moment he releases it, he may 
eat from it, for it is considered trained.  
However, apart from dogs, such as animals hunted by cheetahs, falcons, and similar 
creatures, do not eat from their catch you reach it and perform the proper slaughtering 
(dhabh). This is because Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Mukallibeen' (those 
who train hunting dogs) 
Thus, apart from dogs, their catch is not lawful to eat unless you reach it and slaughter 
it properly." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.315 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.24 • Tafsir Al-Safi, Vol.2 
p.11 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol.4 p.38 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 93, H. 4121
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HUNTING AND SLAUGHTERING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٣، ح ٤١٢١

hadiths.ar:
4123   -  وَّ رَّوَّى هِس َّامُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ ن ْن ِ ج َّالِذٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ كَّلْث ِ إَّلْمَّح ُّوسِى ِّ  
ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّةُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ إَّلْمُّسْلِمُّ ق َّي ُّسَّمِّى  جِي ن َّ ي ُّزْسِلُّهُّ إ َّ ي َّا ْكُّلُّ مَّا إ َّمْسَّك َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّال ي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
And in another narration, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 
"Eat whatever the dog has eaten from, even if it has eaten two-thirds of it. Eat whatever 
the dog has eaten, even if nothing remains of it except a single piece."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.315 • Al-Wafi, Vol.19 p.145 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.23 
p.336 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.16 p.105 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 93, H. 4122
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HUNTING AND SLAUGHTERING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٣، ح ٤١٢٢
